primer.markdown:
# One template, any visual API, any AI generator

The Universal Design Tool Planning Template is not a Canva workflow wearing a disguise. It is a **meta-pattern** — a shape you fill in with your own API names, your own quality targets, and your own user personas. `UNIVERSAL-TEMPLATE-README.md` calls out the move in its second paragraph: the same five-step pipeline (`analyze → research → architect → plan → document`) applies whether you are wiring Claude to Figma plus DALL-E, to Sketch plus Stable Diffusion, or to Adobe Express plus Midjourney. The APIs change. The skeleton does not.

The skeleton is parameterised by **seven variables**. Four are required (`PROJECT_NAME`, `PRIMARY_API`, `SECONDARY_SERVICES`, `TARGET_USERS`) and three are optional with defaults (`SPEED_TARGET: < 30s`, `QUALITY_TARGET: 90%+`, `ARCHITECTURE_PATTERN: two-agent`). Pick values that describe your project, and the workflow produces the same six documents every time: `PROJECT-OVERVIEW.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `API-INTEGRATION.md`, `WORKFLOWS.md`, `README.md`, `PROJECT-SUMMARY.md`.

Why does this matter? Because the costly part of planning a design-automation tool is not choosing the API — it is *structuring* the decision. The template freezes the structure so you can spend your thinking on the variables that actually vary. The CANVA-CODE validation run proved the shape: 22 minutes, 9.18/10 average quality, 91% token efficiency, 3,822 lines of implementation-ready docs. Swap in Figma and DALL-E and you get the same shape.
primer.markdown_es:
# Una plantilla, cualquier API visual, cualquier generador de IA

La Plantilla Universal de Planificación para Herramientas de Diseño no es un flujo de trabajo de Canva disfrazado. Es un **meta-patrón** — una forma que se completa con tus propios nombres de API, tus propios objetivos de calidad y tus propias personas de usuario. El archivo `UNIVERSAL-TEMPLATE-README.md` lo declara en su segundo párrafo: el mismo pipeline de cinco pasos (`analizar → investigar → diseñar → planear → documentar`) aplica tanto si conectas Claude a Figma más DALL-E, a Sketch más Stable Diffusion, o a Adobe Express más Midjourney. Las APIs cambian. El esqueleto no.

El esqueleto está parametrizado por **siete variables**. Cuatro son obligatorias (`PROJECT_NAME`, `PRIMARY_API`, `SECONDARY_SERVICES`, `TARGET_USERS`) y tres son opcionales con valores por defecto (`SPEED_TARGET: < 30s`, `QUALITY_TARGET: 90%+`, `ARCHITECTURE_PATTERN: two-agent`). Eliges valores que describen tu proyecto, y el flujo produce los mismos seis documentos cada vez.

Importa porque la parte costosa de planificar una herramienta de automatización de diseño no es elegir la API — es *estructurar* la decisión. La plantilla congela la estructura para que gastes tu pensamiento en las variables que de verdad varían. La validación de CANVA-CODE probó la forma: 22 minutos, calidad promedio 9.18/10, eficiencia de tokens del 91%, 3.822 líneas de documentación lista para implementar.
primer.key_insight: The APIs change. The skeleton does not. Seven variables parameterize every design-automation project; the five-step pipeline is universal.
primer.key_insight_es: Las APIs cambian. El esqueleto no. Siete variables parametrizan cada proyecto de automatización de diseño; el pipeline de cinco pasos es universal.

visual.alt: A diagram showing the universal template as a cuboctahedron frame with seven labeled vertices (PROJECT_NAME, PRIMARY_API, SECONDARY_SERVICES, TARGET_USERS, SPEED_TARGET, QUALITY_TARGET, ARCHITECTURE_PATTERN) feeding into a five-step pipeline bar (analyze, research, architect, plan, document) that emits six stacked document rectangles labeled PROJECT-OVERVIEW, ARCHITECTURE, API-INTEGRATION, WORKFLOWS, README, and PROJECT-SUMMARY.

sandbox_policy.csp: default-src 'none'; style-src 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; script-src 'unsafe-inline'; img-src data:; font-src https://fonts.gstatic.com; connect-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'self'

interactive.success_criterion: Learner fills in the seven template variables (PROJECT_NAME, PRIMARY_API, SECONDARY_SERVICES, TARGET_USERS, SPEED_TARGET, QUALITY_TARGET, ARCHITECTURE_PATTERN) in a static form and sees the resulting YAML configuration rendered next to it — matching the exact shape shown in UNIVERSAL-TEMPLATE-README.md lines 287-322.

artifact.excerpt:
### Required Variables

```yaml
PROJECT_NAME: "Your project name"
  # Examples: "FigmaAI", "CanvaBot", "SketchAssist"

PRIMARY_API: "Your main visual API"
  # Options: Canva, Figma, Adobe Express, Sketch, InVision, Miro
  # Determines: Visual scaffolding capabilities

SECONDARY_SERVICES: "Your AI generation service(s)"
  # Options: NanoBanana, DALL-E, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, Leonardo.ai
  # Determines: Image/icon/design generation quality and speed

TARGET_USERS: "Your designer personas"
  # Examples: product designers, marketing teams, content creators, freelancers
  # Determines: Workflow design and success metrics
```

### Optional Variables

```yaml
SPEED_TARGET: "Your performance goal"
  # Default: < 30s
  # Options: < 15s (fast), < 30s (balanced), < 60s (quality)

QUALITY_TARGET: "Your quality threshold"
  # Default: 90%+
  # Options: 85%+ (draft), 90%+ (production), 95%+ (premium), 99%+ (enterprise)

ARCHITECTURE_PATTERN: "Your agent design"
  # Default: two-agent
  # Options: single-agent, two-agent, multi-agent
```

questions.prompt: Per UNIVERSAL-TEMPLATE-README.md, which four template variables are marked **Required** (as opposed to optional with defaults)?
questions.explanation: UNIVERSAL-TEMPLATE-README.md §Required Variables (lines 287-302) lists exactly four: PROJECT_NAME, PRIMARY_API, SECONDARY_SERVICES, TARGET_USERS. SPEED_TARGET, QUALITY_TARGET, and ARCHITECTURE_PATTERN are listed in §Optional Variables (lines 304-321) with documented defaults.

questions.prompt: What is the documented default for `SPEED_TARGET` when a project does not override it?
questions.explanation: UNIVERSAL-TEMPLATE-README.md line 308 specifies `# Default: < 30s` for SPEED_TARGET. The fast (< 15s) and quality (< 60s) options are alternatives, not defaults.

questions.prompt: The template's core five-step pipeline, as stated in UNIVERSAL-TEMPLATE-README.md line 22, is:
questions.explanation: UNIVERSAL-TEMPLATE-README.md §Core Pattern (line 22) shows the exact sequence: `[analyze-task-space] → [research-apis] → [design-architecture] → [plan-workflows] → [document-everything]`. This is the universal spine regardless of which APIs are plugged in.
